def: adj. (f. -ī), Wet, damp moist, saturated (especially with rain); running (as a sore).
more: Ped. adj. 1. Wet; moist; saturated, especially with rain. 1. Ib dhartī ālī nā saë, dauthāīṅ uṭh-kai jot lai . W. Rus. The ground is just moist enough, plough it early to-morrow morning. 2. Ālā īṅdhan, ūṅchā chūlhā, tawā niputto bhārī rī. Mūlan aggyā jaltī nāhiṅ, phūṅkat, phūṅkat hārī rī . W. Rus. Song. The fuel's wet, the grate too high, The plate so thick—barren blast it. I've blown and blown, done up am I, The fire wont burn the deuce a bit. 2. Running, full of running matter (a sore).

n. f. S. आलय place. A small recess in a pillar or wall for holding a lamp, etc.; niche ( tāq ); receptacle. 1. Divāl khoī āloṅ ne, ghar khoyā sāloṅ ne . Prov. The niche brought down the wall, the brother in-law the house and all. 2. Āla de nivālā . Prov. Recess! my mess! (What is bred in the bone must come out in the flesh). ☞ The story is told of a beggar girl who pined in the luxurious affluence of a royal seraglio, into which she was admitted, until the king's physician hit upon the expedient of placing her food in a niche in the wall, which she supplicated just as she was wont to beg from persons in the streets in early life.

n. m. 1. The name of a hero or poet. 2. A song about Ālā and Udal , a ballad; heroic poem; epic poetry. 3. The name by which Ṭhags call one another. ālā gā'nā , v. a. 1. To tell one's own story; to blow one's own trumpet. 2. To tell a long story; to dwell on a subject. Kahāṅ kī ālā gāī .

def: n. m. From P. āre, bale yes. Hum and haw. 1. Evasion; shuffling; putting off ( ṭāl maṭol ). 2. Imposition; deception; fraud; deceit. 3. Idleness; inactivity; indolence; sloth. Din khoyā āle bāle, kātan baiṭhī dȳā ujāle . Prov. She 'll/?/ moon about all day, by lamp-light spin away. ālā bālā batā' nā, ṭāle bāle batānā , v. a. 1. To put off; delay; shuffle; evade. Kuchh dete ho na dilāte ho, roz āle bāle batāte ho . 2. To delude; dupe; trick; impose on. Āle bāle batā-ḳe ṭhag lȳā .
adj. Of high rank or degree.
more: adj. 1. Greatest; paramount; pre-eminent; hightest; supreme; eminent; sublime; most high; most exalted. 2. Principal; chief; maximum. 3. Superior; upper; higher ( ūṅchā ); greater ( baṛā ); major. Ālā ālā marātbe! Benediction. ālā darje kā . Of high rank or degree.

def: adj. Unattached, unconnected, disconnected, detached, separated, sundered; disjointed, dislocated; separate, distinct, loose; lonely, solitary; excluded, shut out; liberated, disengaged; free, independent; singular, standing alone, unique; peculiar, strange; placed apart or aside, (hence) secure, safe; — adv. Apart, aloof, asunder; adrift, alone; at a distance, far off or away; at liberty; imperceptibly, unperceived, unseen; — intj. Stand off! away! be gone!
more: adj. S. अ not, लग् to adhere. 1. Disjoined; disconnected; detached; broken or come off; separate. 1. Chūnā divāl se alag ho gayā . 2. Zanjīr kī kaṛī alag ho gaī . 2. Disjointed; dismembered; dislocated. Haḍḍī alag ho gaī . 3. Separated; straggling; cut off; apart; asunder. 1. Dhaṛ se sir alag ho gayā . 2. Kahīṅ nākhūn bhī gosht se alag huā hai? Prov. Flesh and bone (blood relations) cannot be separated. 4. Loose; crumbling; ready to break or fall to pieces. Ye alag hai, ise hāth na lagānā . 5. Unchained; unfettered unshackled. 6. Alone; isolated; solitary; solus ( akelā ); apart; aloof. 7. Excluded; exclusive; exempt; shut out. 1. Sab sadqe, maiṅ alag . Prov. A sacrifice to you be all the world—barring me. 2. Hamārī saṅgat se alag ho gayā . 8. Put out of caste; excommunicated; banished. Zāt se alag ho gayā . 9. Liberated; disengaged; free; loose. Ham to alag haiṅ, apne se kachh hī ho . 1…

adv. 1. Asunder; apart ( judā ); adrift. Ye alag rotī hai, woh alag pīṭtā hai . Here this one weeps alone, there that one beats his head. 2. At a distance; away; far off; at arm's length. Ek hāth meṅ alag jā paṛā . At one stroke his head was severed from his body. 3. Unperceived; imperceptibly; unseen. alag , intj. Away! stand off! be off! begone! keep your distance! Alag! bakiā kyā hai? alag alag , adj. 1. The emphatic form of alag , q. v. Din bhar to alag alag rahe woh, Do waqt se shām ko mile woh . Nasīm. All day disparted from each other, they At evening meet when night doth meet the day. 2. Dissatisfied; displeased; annoyed. Āge to ham se is qadar thā na kabhā alag alag, Ab huī aisī kyā khatā, rahtā hai tū alag alag . Zafar. alag alag , adv. Individually; severally; separately; one by one. Sab kī mazūrī alag alag chukā do. alag paṛ'nā , v. n. To sleep apart from one's wife or husban…
adj. Separate, apart, distinct, loose, aloof, aside, asunder. الگ الگ alag alag, adv. Separately. الگ تهلگ alag thalag, Apart.
subs: adj. emphat. of alag, q.v. Holding aloof; dissatisfied, displeased; — adv. Separately, one by one, severally, individually
adj. Fine, delicate, soft; — adv. Softly, gently, tenderly
(-se), To live away (from); to live apart, live alone; — to hold aloof, refrain (from), keep one's distance; to avoid; to keep out of the way

v.t. To sunder separate, sever, detach, disconnect; to displace, disjoint, dislocate; to set free, extricate, set at liberty, unfasten, unbind; to set apart; to part with, get rid of; to remove; to put away, divorce; to put aside; to exclude; to dispose of; to discriminate; to sift; to put out of the way; to embezzle; to put out of sight; to have done with, to close, wind up (a business, &c.); to give up, relinquish, abandon

v.n. To be separated, severed, &c. (see alag); to part, separate, go asunder; to be divided or partitioned; to be removed; to be got rid of, parted with, disposed of, to be dismissed; to be sifted, picked, selected; to be made away with, be embezzled, carried off, stolen; to become aloof, to back out of, to evade; to withdraw, retire; to slink off, away; to escape, get clean away.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit अलग्न (alagna).

def: separate, distinct; different
wety: From Old Indo-Aryan alagna-.

def: v. n. Add "To be" to alag , adj. q. v. 1. To be divided, partitioned, parcelled out. Dono bhāīyoṅ kā hissa alag ho gayā . 2. To be selected, picked out, segregated. Achchhā saudā alag ho gayā . 3. To be removed, carried away, transported. Yāṅ se asbāb alag ho, to jagah khālī ho . 4. To be disposed of, sold. 5. To be dismissed, discharged, disengaged. 6. To be stolen, carried off, made away with. 7. To escape; steer clear of. Jān chhupā-ke alag ho gayā . 8. To evade; shirk; escape from. 9. To withdraw; go away; take one's way. Yāṅ se alag ho . 10. To discontinue; give over. Karte karte alag ho gayā . ( Double entendre .)

v. a. See utārnā (17, 19, 26, 43.) 1. To separate; detach; part; disconnect. Bahū beṭe ko alag kar dȳā . 2. To unfasten; unchain; unbind; untie ( kholnā ). Jue se bail alag karo . 3. To scatter; disperse; send flying. 4. To let loose; liberate; set free; exempt. Buḍḍhe gulām ko alag kar dȳā 5. To put aside; put away; divorce; get rid of; put on the shelf; dissociate. Yeh bakheṛā alag karo . 6. To exclude; shut out; reject; discard. Phr. To shut the door on one. Apnī maṅḍlī se alag kar dȳā . 7. To weed; winnow; eliminate. Gehūṅ chare alag kar do . 8. To pick ( chunnā ); select; set apart; segregate. 9. To sell; dispose of; part with. Ghoṛā alag karoge? 10. To finish; complete; have done with. Khā pī ke alag karo . 11. To pull; take or pick to pieces; break up. 12. To cause to vanish or disappear; put out of the way. Mujrim ko alag kar dȳā . 13. To put out of sight; secrete; conceal; hide ( chhupānā ). Sāmne se alag kar do . 14. To remove; do away with; steal. Āṅkhoṅ ke sāmne se rupayā alag kar lȳā . 15. To embezzle; appropriate by breach of trust. Parāyā virsā alag kar gaë . 16. To lay by; put by; store up. 17. To shut up shop; break up; wind up. Kārkhānā alag kȳā . 18. To give up; set aside; abandon; resign. Us kām ko alag kȳā

Attachment, connection, dependence, relation, affinity; concern, interest; part; reference, bearing (to), relevancy; commerce (with), intercourse, correspondence, communication; — a depenpendency, a province, division (of territory), district, parish; a tenure, holding; an estate, a manor; jurisdiction; a business, concern; an office, post
more: n. m. علق clung to. See تعلق 1, 3. 1. Dependency; affiliation; correlation; affinity. 2. Part ( sarokār ). 3. Bearing; relevancy; allusion. 4. Tenure; holding. 5. An estate; manor. 6. Province; division; district; circle; parish. 7. Jurisdiction; beat. 8. Office; post. ilāqah uṭh gayā . The connection was severed. ilāqah-band , n. m. A gold-lace worker; a tape-maker ( paṭvā , 1). ilāqah-bandī , n. f. 1. The business of lace-making. 2. Lace-work; gold-lace work. ilāqah rakhnā , v. n. To be allied, or associated with; have affinity with; to be correlated, or dependent on ( tāalluq rakhnā ). ilāqeh se bāhar . Beyond the limits. ilāqa-i-adā'at . The jurisdiction of a court. ilāqeh meṅ . Within the limits, or jurisdiction of.

s. m. 1. Dependence, relation, connexion, interest. 2. Pretension, right. 3. s.f. Gold-lace work; lace-work; braiding; the making of fringe, or tape, &c.; — the business of lace-work, &c.

def: n. m. S. अलक्ष्य from अ not, लक्ष--- to see; hence, invisible. 1. The invisible. A lakh nām sāīṅ kā . Mendicant's cry. 2. Without shape or form (as the Creator). 3. A form of salutation among Jogīs. alakh-purash , n. m. The invisible being. A lakh-purash kī māyā, Kahīṅ dhūp, kahīṅ chhāyā . Prov. The wondrous art of the unseen displayed, Here cheering light, there cool refreshing shade. alakh jagā'nā , v. a. lit . To invoke the invisible. To ask alms in the name of God. 1. Alakh jāge! May God spring in your hearts! 2. Phirūṅ jagālā alakh, maiṅ ṭukṛe māṅg māṅg ke khāūṅ, Sat merā ḍig jāë abhī, jo bachan-hār ho jāūṅ . Play. Sarwān nīr . In God's name will I beg my way and live on crusts of bread, My truth the forfeit is if I depart from what I've said. 3. Ghar ghar alakh jagāë-ke, jāūṅ pȳā ke des . Song. Lāonī . In God's name will I beg my way from door to door, My love to find the country round I will explore. alakh-dhārī, alakh-nāmī , n. m. A mendicant who acknowledges no deity but Brahma ; an āzād .

n. m. 1. Feasting; festivity; good cheer; jollification; revelry; revels; high jinks. 2. Pleasure; clover; sensual enjoyment. 3. Extravagance; waste; dissipation. 4. Ostentation; show; pomp; parade. Bāhar mȳāṅ alalle talalle, ghar meṅ chūhe pakkeṅ . + Wom. Prov. A broad he feasts on cakes and wine, The housefolk left on rats to dine. alalle talalle kar'nā , v. n. 1. To spend one's days and nights in riot and feasting; live luxuriously; enjoy oneself; give oneself up to pleasure; live in clover; live a debauched life. Ham se izhār thā, ki "marte haiṅ," Yāṅ alalle talalle karte haiṅ . Wom. Shauq. They tole me now that you were dying; While here you are yourself enjoying! 2. To squander; waste ( uṛānā , 11). 3. To beguile or while away the time. Woh to alalle talalle karte phirte haiṅ .

def: adv. v.s.v. al, 'the.'
more: adv. 1. Certainly; surely; assuredly; of course; inevitably; necessarily. Jo jāë Kalcutte, 1 khe khāë albatte . Prov. Whoe'er to Calcutta has been, has eaten dirt certes I ween. 2. To be sure; "thou hast said;" exactly; precisely; truly. Badan pe nahīṅ lattā, pān khāeṅ albattā! Prov. Of clothes to his back he has not an ell, Betel lip-redd'ning he sports like a swell! 3. Yes; aye; very well; even so; just so. 4. By all means; so be it; be it so; ex concesso .
adv. Certainly, indeed, positively, absolutely, assuredly, really.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَلْبَتَّه (albatta), from Arabic الْبَتَّةَ (al-battata).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Playful, sportive, wanton; capricious, fanciful, wayward; volatile, sprightly, lively, gay; light, frivolous, senseless, childish, silly; simple, naïve, artless; — free, independent; careless, indifferent; — strange, uncommon, extraordinary, singular, peculiar; — smart, showy, spruce, foppish; engaging, charming, handsome, beautiful
more: adj. S. अलम् abundance, वेल् to go, H. bal a twist. * 1 Khe khānā , 1. To live by rowing (khenā) . 2. To eat dirt. 1. Wayward; capricious; fanciful; humorsome. Albelī nār kahā na māne . 2. Unfettered; free; fearless; independent ( āzād ). 1. Maiṅ to albelī goryā, hamār koū kā kari . E. Rus. Song. 2. Maiṅ mālan albelī, rahūṅ bāg men akelī, Chaman hove gulzār, mahak rahi rāë chaṅbelī . Play. Chaubolā . In me a maiden gardener gay you see, Alone I dwell within this garden free, The flower beds' bloom, the jasmine's scent 'bout me. 3. Careless; indifferent ( be-parvā ); reckless; sans souci . 4. Peculiar; singular; uncommon; unique . 5. Strange; odd; extraordinary ( anokhā ); queer; quaint; fantastic; grotesque. 6. Wild; playful; sportive; skittish. 7. Volatile; lively; sprightly; airy; gay. 8. Light; free and easy; debonair; degagé 9. Irrepressible; rampant; skittish; wanton. 10. Spruce; sm…

n. 1. A wild, wayward, frolicsome, skittish person. 2. A beau ( bāṅkā ); spark; buck; blade Ghar meṅ nahīṅ tāgā, albelā mā/?/ṅge pāgā . Prov. Not a thread in the house, the swell wants a turban. 3. A beauty; a handsome person. Shakal bhūt kī si, nā/?/ṅ Albele Lāl! Prov. As ugly as the devil, and Beauty his name! 4. A fast man, or woman; a man of pleasure; voluptuary; rake; gallant; fille de joie . 5. A simpleton; a fool ( ahmaq ); ninny; noodle. 1. Albelī ne pakāī khīr, dūdh kī jagah ḍālā nīr . Prov. A ninny made of khīr a mess, She water used for milk witless. 2. Aur woh hotiyāṅ haiṅ albelī, Maiṅ nahīṇ aisī kachchī goliyāṅ khelī . Shauq. Others no doubt there are of simple mind; In me, be sure, no simpleton you'll find.
adj. Artless, simple, innocent. s. m. A fop, a beau. (fem. البیلي A belle).
subs: (f. -ī), A wanton, or capricious, &c. man (or woman); a beau, fop; voluptuary, rake, roué; simpleton, fool, noodle
s.f. A fast woman; a belle; a silly woman, &c.

def: n. m. 1. Wildness; giddiness; sportiveness; frolic. 2. Waywardness; capriciousness; humour; whimsey. 3. Sprightliness; gaiety; gaiete' de cœur ; friskiness. 4. Wantonness; skittishness; lasciviousness. 5. Airs; blandishments; coquettishness. Mujh ko bhātā hai terā khelāpan, Wāh! kyā khūb yeh albelā-pan! Wom. Rangīn. Your playful airs are sweet to me, Your simple freedoms fair to see! 6. Spruceness; smartness; jauntiness. 7. Charm; fascination; winsomeness. 8. Beauty; prettiness; loveliness; elegance. 9. Simplicity; artlessness; innocence. 10. Childishness; puerility; silliness. 11. Freedom; independence; a negligé air. 12. Carelessness; heedlessness; recklessness. 13. Singularity; peculiarity; character. 14. Quaintness; oddity; grotesqueness.

s.m. Playfulness, sportiveness, wantonness, sprightnness, gayety, frolic; capriciousness, waywardness; giddiness, frivolity, levity, silliness, childishness; simplicity, artlessness, innocence; — carelessness, indifference; strangeness, oddness, singularity, peculiarity; — smartness, spruceness, foppishness; airs, blandishments, coquettishness; charm, fascination; beauty, prettiness, elegance.

s.m. Playfulness, sportiveness, wantonness, sprightnness, gayety, frolic; capriciousness, waywardness; giddiness, frivolity, levity, silliness, childishness; simplicity, artlessness, innocence; — carelessness, indifference; strangeness, oddness, singularity, peculiarity; — smartness, spruceness, foppishness; airs, blandishments, coquettishness; charm, fascination; beauty, prettiness, elegance.
more: s. m. Foppishness, spruceness, airs and blandishments.

ety: S. अलम्+वृत्; but cf. albelā, to which albeṭ may be related
def: s.f. Turn, twist, bend, winding; (cf. lapeṭ)
n. f. S. वे weave, H. baṭ twist. A turn; twist; winding; convolution. Rassī kī albeṭ khul gaī. albeṭ de'nā , v. a. To twist; twine; wind.

adj. & s.m. = albelā, q.v. adj. subs: (f. -ī), A wanton, or capricious, &c. man (or woman); a beau, fop; voluptuary, rake, roué; simpleton, fool, noodle
s.f. A fast woman; a belle; a silly woman, &c.

def: adj. & adv. Foolish enough, very foolish or ridiculous, senseless, absurd; useless, fruitless, vain.
more: adv. Useless; of no use; vain; profitless; fruitless; inoperative. Bahre āge gāonā, aur gūṅge āge gal, Andhe āge nāchnā, tīnoṅ al bilal . Prov. To sing to the deaf, as to talk to the dumb, and withal, To dance to the blind, are three things useless all.

ety: burz = Zend barez; from barez = S. barh, cf. al-wand
def: s.m. The highest mountain in the Caucasus; also other high mountains in Hamadān, &c.
more: s. m. Name of a celebrated mountain between Persia and India.

def: one who catches or seizes
wety: From Chagatai آلچی (alçı)

def: v.n. To cry out, scream, bawl, &c.
more: v. n. (dakh.) To scream, to shriek, to cry or bawl out, to bellow ( Harris ).

def: s.m. Humming and hawing; putting off, shuffling, evasion, subterfuge, deception, fraud, deceit; — procrastination; inactivity, idleness, sloth
subs: v.t. To put off, shuffle, evade; to deceive, delude, trick.

adj. That cannot be written; that cannot be taken account of.
adj. Careless, indifferent, heedless, thoughtless, inconsiderate, imprudent, improvident.
more: adj. S. अलोल् unagitated. 1. Nonchalant; careless; heedless. 2. Thoughtless; inconsiderate; improvident. Woh to apne mā bāp par alel hai .

adj. Thousand. (Syn. hazār.)

more: s. m. A kind of dress resembling a shirt without sleeves and open at the sides, worn by fakīrs.

more: 1. s. f. See الفا alfā. 2. adj. Covered with marks like the letter alif (cloth, &c.).

def: adj. Cor. of T. yalg/?/ār , Too much; very much; a great deal. Dāl meṅ algāroṅ namak ḍāl dȳā .
adj. Separate, apart, distinct; free, loose, unconfined, &c. (see alag): — s.m. A sandal.
more: 1. adj. Separate, apart, distinct, free, loose, unconfined. 2. s. m. A sandal.

def: s.f. Separation; holding or keeping aloof, non-interference; being silent.
God only knows what is hidden; God knows ( Khudā jāne ); God is omniscient.
v.t. = alag karnā, q.v.s.v. alag; to put on one side; to put up, raise.
more: E. for alag k . q. v. To lift up; help to raise; uplift; hoist. Baṛe bikhau bikhdhar ko, chalat sīs niwāë, Thoṛe bikhau bichchhū ko, chalat dum algāë . E. Prov. The deadlier-fanged snake doth creep with bended head, The scorpion milder-venomed with his tail upraised.
v. a. To separate, disjoin, to put on one side.

s. m. A small flagelet (a span long, Qanoon-e-islam ), a flute.

def: s.m. The name of a certain Hindū hero, &c. = ālā, q.v.
more: n. prop. Name of a Hindū soldier and poet, from whom a species of poetry takes its name.

def: s.f. The name of a rāginī or musical mode.
more: s. f. Name of a rāginī.

def: adv. 1. In truth; assuredly; verily; truly ( sach ); sooth to say. 2. Actually; indeed; no doubt ( be-shak ). al-haq , intj. O yes! aye! exactly! surely! indeed!
more: adv. In truth, really, the truth, assuredly.

def: Ped. adv. See abhī .
more: adv. Now, at present, presently, just now.

def: n. f. The first sūrat in the Koran. al-hamd paṛh-kar phūṅk'nā , v. a. To read the first chapter of the Qurān and then blow upon the face of a person to protect him from supernatural influences. al-hamdo lillāh , intj. Thank God! Heaven be praised!
God be praised.
s.f. pl. (of لحن laḥn), Notes, tunes, melodies, &c. (see next).
v.n. To rejoice; to be cheerful.
more: v. n. To rejoice, to be cheerful.

def: s.m. The bumble bee, &c. (see ali); — s.f. A female friend, &c. (see ālī).

ety: S. अवर्ति, ava+r̤iti, rt. r̤i; cf. the H. form आवली
def: s.f. Misfortune; great danger; risk of life. (cf. alīk and alīh,' 'an unpleasant thing.')
more: n. f. Mortal danger; risk of life; crisis. alī ṭal'nā , v. n. To escape from the jaws of death.
1. s. m. A large black bee, fabled to be enamoured of the lotus. 2. s. f. (for آلي ālī ) A female friend.

def: s.m. A large black bee, the bumble-bee; spirituous liquor; — s.f. A woman's female friend ( = ālī).
adj. Of or belonging to the āl tree (see āl)
subs: s.m. A colour extracted from the al tree, āl-colour.

def: adj. High (in rank, &c.), eminent, noble; — s.m. The name of Moḥammad's son-in-law (he was, according to the Sunnī sect, the fourth Khalīfa or successor of Moḥammad; but the Shīʻas make him the direct successor, not acknowledging the three other Caliphs); a proper name
more: n. Muhammad's son-in-law. alī-band . 1. An amulet or charm against sorceries or enchantments. 2. A jewel; ornament. alī-dhat , adj. Gigantic; monstrous ( baṛā 1, 2). alī-madad , n. 1. A trick in wrestling. 2. A return salutation. yā alī-madad , Help me Alī!

prep. On; upon. al-al-ittisāl , adv. In a series; in succession; consecutively; seriatim . al-al-ijmāl o al-al-infirād , adv. Jointly and severally. al-al-itlāq , adv. Absolutely; solely. al-al-infirād , adv. Severally. al-at-tavātur , adv. In succession; repeatedly; successively. al-al-hisāb , adv. Suspense account. al-al-hisāb denā , v. a. To make advances to be adjusted afterwards; make payments on account. al-al-khusūs , adv. Particularly; especially. al-ad-davām , adv. Permanently; in perpetuity; for ever. al-as-sabāh , adv. In the morning. al-al-umūm, umūman , adv. In general; generally ( sādhāran , 1.). ala-qadr-i-marātib According to rank, or degree.

adj. High, eminent, noble; a proper name, especially of Muḥammadʼs son-in-law. ¶ aﻌlạ̄, adv. On, upon, according to, above, to, near. علي الاتصال aﻌlạ-l-ittiṣāl, Continually, without intermission. علي الاطلاق aﻌlạ-l-it̤lāḳ, Absolutely, solely. علي الخصوص aﻌlạ-l-ḵẖuṣūṣ, In particular. علي الدوام aﻌlạ-d-dawām, In perpetuity, for ever. علي الرغم aﻌlạ-r-rag̠ẖm, In spite, in opposition. علي الصباح aﻌlạ-ṣ-ṣabāḥ, In the morning. علي هذا القياس aﻌlạ hāẕa-l-ḳiyās, On this measure, in like manner. عليه aﻌlaihi, On him. عليه الرحمه aﻌlaihi-r-raḥma, On him (be) the mercy (of God). عليه السلام aﻌlaihi-s-salām, Blessing on him! عليـﮩم aﻌlaihim, On them. ¶ aﻌlạ̄, s. See علا aﻌlā.
subs: s.m. ʻAlī's tie'; an amulet or charm (against sorceries or enchantments); an ornament, a jewel
s.f. 'Alī's help'; a return salutation; — name of a trick in wrestling.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَلِی (alī), from Arabic عَلِيّ (ʕaliyy).

def: adj. Elevated; eminent; sublime (اونچا 1, 5). ālī-janāb, ālī-jāh . Your highness; your excellency. ālī-khāndān , adj. Of noble birth. ālī-dimāg , n. m. A great intellect. ālī-shān , adj. 1. Splendid; magnificent. 2. Grand; majestic; stately. Kaisā ālī-shān makān banāyā hai! ālī-zarf , adj. Able; gifted. ālīmartaba, ālīqadar , High rank or dignity. ālī-himmat , adj. Aspiring ( ālī-hauslah , under hauslah ). Ālī himmat, sadā muflis . Prov. Great enterprise, always poor. ālī-himmatī , n. f. 1. Highmindedness. 2. Ambition. mizāj-i-ālī , How is your honor?
Aliabad (a city in Hunza district, Gilgit-Baltistan, Pakistan)
wety: From علی (’alī) + ـآباد (-ābād).

def: a male given name from Chagatai
wety: From Chagatai علیقلی (ʿly-qly, literally “slave of [Imam] Ali”); compare Azerbaijani Əliqulu, Turkish Alikulu.

def: s. m. A colour extracted from the āl tree (Morinda citrifolia).

s.m. The bumble-bee; a scorpion ( = ali); — s.f. A woman's female friend; — row, range, continuous line; bed (of flowers or vegetables); ridge, mound of earth crossing ditches or dividing fields ( = meṅṛ), dike, embankment; — a land measure, equivalent to four bīsīs.
more: n. f. S. आलि. 1. A young female friend or companion to a woman. Mohe ḍagar chalat dīnī gārī Rasyā ne, dekho morī ālī! Song. Ṭhumrī . This amorous swain did on the road treat me, my dear, To prurient sensuous language—quite unfit to hear! 2. A land measure, equivalent to four bīsīs .
s. f. A female friend, a damsel. ¶ आलय ālay, s. m. House, habitation.

ety: for A. عالٍ ʻāliṉ, act. part. of علو 'to be high,' &c.
def: adj. High, elevated, exalted, eminent, noble, grand, sublime
more: (part. act. of علو) adj. High, sublime, eminent, grand. (Fem. عاليه āﻌliya ). عالي تبار āﻌlī-tabār, adj. Of high descent. عالي جاه āﻌlī-jāh, or عاليشان āﻌlī-shān, adj. Of high rank, magnificent. عالي جاهي āﻌlī-jāh-ī, s. f. Highness of rank or dignity. عالي فطرت āﻌlī-fit̤rat, adj. High-minded. عالي قدر āﻌlī-ḳadr, adj. Worthy, of high rank. عالي مرتبه āﻌlī-martaba, adj. High in rank or dignity. عاليمقام āﻌlī-maḳām, adj. Of high rank. عالي مقدار āﻌlī-miḳdār, adj. Exalted, high in dignity. عالي منش āﻌlī-manish, or عالي همت āﻌlī-himmat, High-minded, ambitious. عالي منشي āﻌlī-manish-ī, s. f. Magnanimity. عالي همتي āﻌlī-himmat-ī, s. f. High-mindedness, ambition.
subs: adj. Of high descent, of noble lineage
or ʻālī-janāb, adj. Of high rank, of exalted dignity; — your Highness, your Excellency
s.f. Highness of rank or dignity
adj. Of a high family, of a noble stock
adj. Of high rank or dignity; magnificent, splendid; stately, grand, majestic
or ʻālī-martaba, or ʻālī-maqām, or ʻālī-miqdār, or ʻālī-manqabat, or ālī-makān, adj. Of high rank or dignity
or ʻālī-himmat, adj. High-minded, magnanimous; of an aspiring nature, ambitious
or ʻālī-himmatī, s.f. High-mindedness, magnanimity; ambition.
wety: From Arabic عَالٍ (ʕālin).

def: s.m. The first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets
more: n. m. The first letter of the Arabic and Persian alphabet. 1. Alif ke nām be nahīṅ jāntā . Prov. He cannot distinguish A. from B. 2. Alif ke nām khutkā nahīṅ jānte . Prov. He knows not ا (A) from a stick. alif Allāh , adj. 1. The alif of the word Allāh . i. e. alone; having no friends or relations. (Said by one who is deserted or alone). 2. Poor; penniless; destitute. alif be , n. f. A B C; the alphabet. al'fan, sūkhī alfān , n. f. A tall, lean, slender woman. al'af ho'nā , v. n. 1. To rear (as a horse). 2. To be erect ( double entendre ).
subs: 'The alif of (the word) allāh'; adj. Alone, friendless, having no friend save God; destitute, poor
The mark of the letter alif made by faqīrs on the forehead (indicative of their separation from worldly concerns, &c.)
s.f. The a, b, c, the alphabet
v.n. To become erect; to rear (as a horse).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian الف (alif), from Arabic أَلِف (ʔalif).

subs: or ʻālim-ě-g̠aib, adj. & s.m. Prescient, knowing the past and the future, or the visible and the invisible; omniscient; — the One who knows the visible and the invisible, the Omniscient.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَالَم (ālam), borrowed from Arabic عَالَم (ʕālam), borrowed from Aramaic עָלְמָא or Classical Syriac ܥܳܠܡܳܐ.

def: adj. Speaking barbarously; stammering, stuttering (cf. luknat).
s.f. Inactivity, sluggishness, sloth, &c. = ālas, q.v. (cf. askat). s.m. Inactivity, lack of energy, indolence, laziness, idleness, sloth; drowsiness; — adj. Lazy, slothful, indolent; drowsy.
more: E. n. S. अ not, लस् to move. 1. Inactivity; inaction; idleness; sloth; laziness; indolence; inertness; supineness; sluggishness; langour. 2. Dilatoriness; procrastination; dawdling. 3. Sleepiness; drowsiness; heaviness; nodding.

def: v.n. = alsānā, q.v. v.n. To be slack, slow, sluggish, lazy; to doze, be drowsy or sleepy; to rest; to be fatigued, wearied, done up.
more: v. n. To be tired, See alsānā .

def: s.f. Inactivity, sluggishness, sloth, &c. = ālas, q.v. (cf. askat). s.m. Inactivity, lack of energy, indolence, laziness, idleness, sloth; drowsiness; — adj. Lazy, slothful, indolent; drowsy.
s.f. = ālkas, q.v.
more: E. adj. 1. Inactive; indolent; lazy; slothful; idle; remiss; inert; supine; sluggish; languid; heavy; dull; slow. Phr. With folded arms; with hands in pocket. 2. Dilatory; laggard; lagging; dawdling.
subs: &c. = ālsī and askatī, q.q.v. s.f. Inactivity, sluggishness, sloth, &c. = ālas, q.v. (cf. askat). s.m. Inactivity, lack of energy, indolence, laziness, idleness, sloth; drowsiness; — adj. Lazy, slothful, indolent; drowsy.

def: v. a. A. قط has been cut. 1. To cut; break off; cut short; discontinue. 2. To bring to an end; to put a stop to. Aísī dostī ko alqat karte haiṅ . A truce to such friendship. 3. To turn off, out, or away; dismiss; dispense with. Naukar ko alqat kar dȳā . 4. To settle, balance, or square accounts with; have done with; get rid of. De le-kar alqat karo . 5. Not to admit; refuse to accept; reject. Yeh sher alqat . This verse wont pass (Said in capping verses).
s.m. Cutting off, severing, sundering; adj. Put aside, dismissed; rejected; inadmissible
subs: v.t. To cut off, sever, sunder, break off; to cut short, bring to an end; to put away, dismiss, get rid of; to adjust, settle, balance, wind up (an account, &c.).

def: n. f. S. अलक a curl, lock. A row of side-curls.
s.f. A coat with sleeves, a surtout (like the qabā, but having buttons, instead of strings, at the neck, breast, and navel. — Some, however, affirm that the garment is worn under the qabā.
more: s. f. A coat, vest, garment.
s. f. A garment (as the قبا ḳabā, q. v. but having buttons instead of strings, and that in three places, at the neck, navel, and between the two: Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: adv. In a word; in short; in fine; in fact; at last; at long and last. Phr. To sum up; to make a long story short.
more: adv. In short, in a word.

def: alcohol
wety: Borrowed from English alcohol, with influence from Arabic الْكُحْل (al-kuḥl).

def: goodbye; farewell
wety: An Arabised rendition of خُدا حافِظ (xudā hāfiz), in which the Classical Persian خُدَا (xudā, “god”) is replaced by the Arabic اللّٰه (allāh, “God; Allah”). First attested in c. 19th century, but only came into common usage in Pakistan in the 1980s.

def: a male given name from Chagatai
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian and Chagatai اللهقلی (allāh-qulī, literally “slave of God”); compare Azerbaijani Allahqulu, Turkish Allahkulu.

ety: i.e. al ilāh, 'the God,' by way of eminence
def: s.m. God, the Supreme Being; — intj. O God! Have mercy!
more: Hin. n. m. ال The, الَه God. The Being who exists necessarily, by himself, comprising all the attributes of perfection. God; the Supreme Being. Allah kā diyā sab kuchh . Prov. allāh! intj. O Lord! O God! have mercy on us! allāh'o ak'bar! intj. 1. God is great!—recited at every standing, sitting, and kneeling in prayer, and also when slaughtering an animal. 2. An exclamation of surprise or wonder. Allāho akbar! kitnā baṛā qilā hai! allāh' allāh'! intj. 1. An exclamation of surprise and approbation. 2. By God! By Jove! wonderful! excellent! 3. Thank God! heaven be praised! Allāh Allāh, khair sallāh . Prov. Thank God, it is over. (All's well that ends well). allāh allāh karnā , v. n. To take the name of God; to count one's beads. Allāh Allāh karo, khair māṅgo . Prov. Call on God, his blessing ask. allāh āmīṅ! intj. God grant! so be it! Heaven protect him! allāh āmīṅ , adv. With prayer and f…

s. m. God. الله اکبر allāhu akbar, God is great. الله الله allāh allāh! An interjection expressive of surprise and approbation. الله ري allāh re, O God! my God! اللہم صل علي محمد allāhumma ṣalli aﻌlạ muḥāmmadiṉ, God be gracious to Muhammad! (an ejaculation expressive of approbation on tasting or smelline any thing very pleasant).
subs: God is great (an introductory ejaculation used in prayer, and also when slaughtering an animal; and an exclamation of surprise, wonder, resignation, &c.: it is called the takbīr)
intj. (expressive of surprise, approbation, &c.), Good God! good gracious! wonderful! extraordinary! excellent! thank God!
To call on God; to say (one's) prayers, count (one's) beads
intj. God grant it be so! So be it! Heaven protect him; — adv. With prayer and fasting, &c.; with great care, anxiously; — allāh āmīn-kā laṛkā, A child granted after many prayers
(for allāh-wālī), intj. God preserve you! God speed! Adieu!
To propitiate God and the saints (by offerings, &c.); to make a religious vow
The name of God; — nothing whatever, not a rap
To take the name of God, to have the fear of God (in one, — said to one who tells a lie, e.g. allāh-kā nām lo!)
lit. 'The light of God'; a gift from God (as a beard, &c); a very beautiful person; (ironically) a hideously ugly person; a very wicked person
An innocent, a demented person, lunatic; simpleton, fool
The soul; life; the animal kingdom; — a poor fellow, poor creature
allāh-log, s.m. God's people, holy men, good men; mendicants; careless or thoughtless persons; — simple-minded people, guileless people
s.f. lit. 'One stricken by God'; A God-forsaken wife; a gadding wife; an unchaste woman, a harlot
s.f. An artless, simple-minded person; a simpleton; — a snail (Helix)
intj. O God! God alone is stable and sure! Good God! (an ejaculation expressing surprise, &c. = allāh re); — a salutation among faqīrs.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَللّٰه (allāh), from Arabic اللّٰه (allāh). First attested as Old Hindi अलह (alaha).

def: Allahu akbar; oh my God
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اللّٰهُ أَكْبَر (allāhu ʔakbar, “God is greater”)

ety: intens. n. fr. علم; see ʻilm
def: adj. Very knowing, very scientific, very learned; all-knowing, omniscient; — s.m. The Omniscient
more: adj. superl. (of علم) Most knowing, omniscient. علام الغيوب aﻌllāmu-l-g̠ẖuyūb, Knowing all mysteries (an epithet of God).
subs: adj. & s.m. Knowing all mysteries, acquainted with things invisible, an epithet of the Deity.

ety: for A. علامة, fr. ʻallām, q.v.
def: adj. Very knowing, most learned ( = ʻallām); — s.m. & f. A very learned man or woman; — a very knowing or artful woman
subs: (also ʻallāmatuʼd-dahr), or ʻallāma-e-aṣr (also ʻallāmatuʼl-ʻaṣr), s.m. The most learned of the age.

def: s.m. Nonsense, rubbish, trash; toying, frivolity; pretext, idle excuse; misappropriation, embezzlement; — adv. Nonsensically, without consideration, without discrimination, at random
more: n. m. S. alīkam , Pr. aliam False. 1. Trash; stuff; hash; mess; mash. Roṭī nahīṅ khātā, din bhar allam gallam chartā rahtā hai . 2. Rubbish ( ākhor ). 3. Nonsense ( aṛ baṛ ). 4. E. Peculation; embezzlement; misappropriation. allam gallam , adv. At random; without discrimination ( anṭ kī sanṭ ). allam gallam baknā , v. n. To talk absurdly, foolishly; to talk nonsense. allam gallam kar'nā , E. v. a. To make away with; embezzle; misappropriate. allam gallam khā'nā , v. a. To eat every thing—good, bad, and indifferent. Allam gallam khāëgā, to dard pahle hogā .
s. m. Trifling talk or business, toying, wickedness, mischievousness.
subs: v.n. To talk nonsense, to prate absurdly, or at random
v.t. To misappropriate embezzle; to hocus-pocus
v.t. To eat all kinds of trash.

ety: A. الل alal, rt. الّ alla; cf. H. alalānā
def: v.n. To cry out (with pain), to groan, wail; to bawl, scream, shriek.
more: v. n. 1. To scream; squeak; squall; squeal. 2. To shriek; groan; wail.
v. n. To bawl, to scream, to squeak.

def: adj. See الّهر.

def: adj. Boyish, childish; young; raw, inexperienced, inexpert; thoughtless; untaught, ignorant; untrained, undisciplined; — s.m. Youth, child, a mere boy (in contempt); young (of an animal), colt, &c.

def: adj. = allaṛ, q.v. adj. Boyish, childish; young; raw, inexperienced, inexpert; thoughtless; untaught, ignorant; untrained, undisciplined; — s.m. Youth, child, a mere boy (in contempt); young (of an animal), colt, &c.

subs: s.f. Childish talk, silly prattle; childishness, folly; trifling employment; frivolity.

n. f. Sleep ( nīṅd ). Ājā rī, allo jhallo, bābū ko khilāo, Ādhī roṭī, ḍeṛh karelā, ṭikulī mahīnā . Nursery Song. Lorī . Come Sleep! my baby's eyelids steep! One half a cake for you I'll keep, One and a half Karela cheap, And monthly one rupee I'll tip.
E. v. a. To embezzle; appropriate fraudulently.

def: s.f. Chest of drawers; book-case, press, wardrobe, cabinet.
more: n. f. A chest of drawers; book-case; cabinet.
wety: Borrowed from Portuguese almario or armário, from Latin armarium (“closet, chest”), from arma (“weapons, tools”) + -ārium (“denoting a place where things are kept”).

more: n. m. A diamond; adamant. ilmās-tarāsh' , Ped. for H. hīrā-kaṭ , adj. Cut into facets (as glass, stone, etc.).
s. m. A diamond, adamant. الماس تراش almās-tarāsh, 1. s. m. Diamond-dust. 2. adj. Cut into angles (as glass).
subs: s.m. Diamond-cutter; a diamond used in cutting other gems or glass; diamond-dust; — adj. Cut into angles or facets (as glass, &c.).

more: adj. Cut into facets (like a diamond). الماسي رنگ almāsī rang, s. m. A bright clear white colour.

def: adj. Intoxicated, drunk; — lustful, lascivious, wanton; — infuriated; mad, insane.
more: adj. 1. Drunk; intoxicated; in liquor. 2. Wanton; lustful; lascivious; skittish. Yeh aurat to baṛī almast ho gaī hai . 3. Mad; insane; crazy. See albelā (3).

def: pandemic
wety: عالمی ('ālmī, “worldwide, global”) + وبا (vabā, “epidemic”)

def: Praise to God! good God! (an ejaculation expressive of surprise, fear, denial, &c.).
adj. Twice; double; twofold ( dūnā ).
s.m. A portion of unripe corn.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀆𑀮𑀼𑀕 (āluga), from Sanskrit आलुक (āluka, “elephant foot yam”). The word began to be used in the sense "potato" after the importation of potatoes to South Asia from the Americas by Europeans, displacing cultivation of the similar elephant foot yam.

criticism; review; opinion

wety: From Sanskrit.

def: s.m. Disappearance; invisibility.
s.m. Looking, seeing; sight.
s.m. Gambol (especially of a horse), caper, frisking; frolic, playfulness; wantonness, dalliance, toying
more: n. f. S. लल् to play. 1. Gambols (generally said of a colt, etc); capers; frolic; playfulness. 2. Wantonness; dalliance; toying. alol kar'nā , v. a. To skip; frisk; caper; gambol; frolic. Sawār gir paṛeṅ sote meṅ chārpāī se, Kare jo khuāb meṅ ghoṛā unhoṅ ke nīche alol . A satire on the King's Cavalry. Saudā. Though in a dream perchance his horse should frisk and bound, Thrown off his bed the rider sprawls upon the ground.
1. (for आ and लोल Shaking, moving) s. f. Gambol (generally applied to a horse). 2. (for अ and लोल ) adj. Steady.
subs: To gambol, caper, frisk, skip, frolic

def: adj. Not shaking, steady, firm.
(see الول) s. f. Gambols, playsomeness, wantonness.
adj. = alaukik, q.v. adj. Not of this world, supernatural, super-human, transcendental; unknown, uncommon, strange, rare, extraordinary, not current; unpopular.
more: adj. S. अलौकिक. Never seen before; unknown; strange; extraordinary. Ek alolak maiṅ suno, mere rāvālyā, Kānī kājar de, bhalī mere rāvalyā! A thing was told to me, husband, so odd and quaint— A woman with one eye her sightless eye did paint.

def: adj. Without salt; insufficiently salted; fresh; tasteless, insipid.
more: adj. S. अ not, लवण salt. Without salt; without enoughsalt; tasteless; insipid. 1. Āj kā sālan alonā hai . 2. Mere ke Rām Jī āë, Bathuā sāg alonī bhājī, Ruchi ruchi bhog lagāë . Bhajan. Ram Jī came to my house a guest, Some coarse pot-herb saltless I dressed, The god fell to the meal with zest.
adj. Not salt, fresh, saltless, insipid.

def: part. adj. Disappeared; concealed, hidden; retired; out of sight; invisible, unseen, imperceptible, undiscernible; run out; defaced, destroyed
more: adj. S. लुप् to disappear. 1. Unseen; invisible; hidden. 2. Undiscernible; latent; occult. alop anjan , n. m. A collyrium. When applied to the eyes it is supposed to render the man invisible. alop' mā'yā , n. f. 1. The mysteries of nature; the imperceptible power in natural phenomena. 2. The latent power of God working in creation. alop honā, alop ho jānā , v. n. 1. To be concealed; become invisible. 2. To vanish; disappear; to be out of sight. Ahe! sab alop bhelthī deotvā re kī . Mag. Song. Jatāsārī . 3. To cease to be; to be extinct. Gaṅgā jī ke alop hone meṅ thoṛe din rahe haiṅ . Hin. The virtue that is in the Ganges will be extinct in a short while. ☞ The Hindus believe that after 24 years the miraculous virtue of the Ganges will cease.
adj. 1. (for आ and लोप Cutting off, disappearing) Concealed, defaced, retired, run out, destroyed. 2. (for अ and लोप ) Apparent.
subs: s.m. A collyrium, which, applied to the eyes of any one, renders him or her invisible
s.f. The mysteries of nature; the invisible power in nature
v.n. To be concealed; to become invisible; to disappear, vanish, retire, get out of sight; to cease to be, become extinct.

def: Not concealed or hidden; visible, apparent.
v.t. To conceal, hide; — v.n. To lie hid, be concealed.
more: v. a. To conceal, to hide oneʼs self, to lie hid.

def: v.t. To stir, mix; to churn ( = bilonā, q.v.). v.t. To stir, shake, agitate, to churn.
s.m. A screen ( = oṭ, q.v.). s.f. Anything to be gained, advantage, gain, profit; overplus, surplus, saving; abatement, economy; recovery (from sickness), convalescence

adj. Of little weight or measure; of little authority; resting on little evidence

adj. Of little strength, weak ( = alp-bal)
adj. Short-winded; — s.m. A short-winded person
s.m. A little, a little merely; a short time, a few moments
adj. Of little price, of small value
alpa-yaś, adj. Of little note, not known to fame, obscure; — s.m. Limited notoriety or fame.

def: adj. Inactive; apathetic; drowsy; used up.
v.n. To be slack, slow, sluggish, lazy; to doze, be drowsy or sleepy; to rest; to be fatigued, wearied, done up.
more: v. n. 1. To be fatigued, tired, weary, done up. Kām karte karte hāth pair alsā gaë . 2. To be slack, slow, sluggish, or lazy. Tā kā nām let alsāno, Māyā meṅ bhūl rahe apno . Song. Too slow His name to take, forgotten all for Mammon's sake. 3. To doze; to be drowsy or sleepy. Sārī rain taṛpat bītī. arsānī morī aṅkhiyāṅ, Butā do, saīyāṅ, kahāṅ gaṅwāī sārī ratiyāṅ? Wom. Song. Dād/?/ā Mine eyelids droop with sleep, all night I restless lay, Tell me, my husband, where the night you passed away?
v. n. To doze, to be drowsy, to slacken.

n. f. S. अलम् abundance, H. al much, saṭ adhere. See uljhāo (6, 8, 10). 1. Sham; gammon; humbug ( dhāṅdal ). 2. Deception; fraud; double dealing; duplicity. Nikāl apne tū pāoṅ kuchh peṭ se, Mujhe le saṅbhāl iski alseṭ se . Inshā. 3. Unlawful attachment; liason . 4. Difference; discrepancy (in account). Uske hisāb meṅ das rupaye kī alseṭ hai .

ety: prob. by Met. for laseṭ, S. लस+इत
def: s.f. Astuteness, sharpness, knavery, roguery, trickery, humbug, gammon, duplicity, deception, fraud; illicit connection, liaison; deficiency (in an account).

adj. 1. Deceptive; artful; crafty. 2. Slothful; indolent; lazy; tardy; sluggish. Bharī barsāt meṅ āb-dust na lewe, woh bhaṛwā alseṭī hai . Prov. A lazy rascal who wont wash after easing himself, with abundance of water in the height of the rains. 3. Slow in paying one's debts.

adj. & s.m. Astute, artful, crafty; deceitful, fraudulent, backward in paying debts; — lazy, indolent, slothful (these meanings point to alsaṭ as the source); — An artful, crafty fellow, &c.; a lazy rascal.
s.f. Linseed, Linum usitatissimum ( = tīsī)
more: E. n. f. S. अतसी. Linseed; Linum usitatissimum . Wat. alsī kā tel , n. m. Linseed oil.

s.m. Lac-dye; Cotton strongly impregnated with lac-dye, ready to be used for dyeing (used by Hindū women for staining their feet red).
more: W. n. m. S. अलक्त. Cotton strongly impregnated with lac-dye ready for dyeing, used by Hindū women for staining their feet red.

adj. 1. Inverted; upside down; wrong. Phr. Wrong side up; topside t'other way. Ulṭī ṭopī, guṛ chane . Boys' chaff. Your cap's the wrong side out, jaggery and gram fork out. 2. Reversed; contrary; opposite. 3. Contrary to the laws of nature. 4. Stupid; idiotic; brainless. 5. Wrong; incorrect; erroneous. ulṭā bhāṛā , n. Return hire. ulṭā pardā , n. m. Opp. of sīdhā pardā , The cut of an aṅgarkhā to the left hand side, as worn by Hindūs. ul'ṭā pul'ṭā, ulaṭ pulaṭ , adj. 1. Subverted; inverted; topsy turvy; upside down; inside out. 2. Disordered; deranged; confused; irregular. 3. Involved; tangled; jumbled. ulṭā phirnā , v. n. To return; retrace one's steps. ulṭā dhaṛā bāṅdhnā , v. n. To bring a cross suit or counter action.

adv. 1. On the contrary; per contra; vice versa . 1. Ulṭe bāṅs Barelī ko! Prov. Bamboos to Bareilly! (Coals to Newcastle). 2. Ulṭā chor kutvāle ḍāṅḍe! Prov. The thief threatens the constable! 3. Ulṭī Gaṅgā bahnā! Prov. The Ganges flowing upwards! 2. In spite of; notwithstanding. 3. Causelessly; wrongly; unfairly; unjustly. Ulṭi ṭāṅgeṅ gale meṅ paṛīṅ . Prov. His own neck was entagled in his legs, i. e. He was caught in his own trap. 4. On the other hand. ulṭā pulṭā kar'nā, ulaṭ pulaṭ—,ulṭā'nā pulṭā'nā , v. a. 1. To turn over and over again; revolve. 2. To put into disorder; disarrange. 3. To disperse; scatter. Gadar ne ek ālam ko ulaṭ pulaṭ kar dȳā. ulṭā tavā, yā kaṛhāī , n. An inverted frying pan, regarded as an ill omen if brought before one just setting out on a journey. Hin. + Wom. ulṭā tavā , adj. Very black; coal-black ( kālākoëlā ). ulṭā javāb' , n. m. A crooked or impudent answer…
s. m. Cotton strongly impregnated with the die of lac, ready to be used for dying, &c. (principally used by the Hindū women for staining their feet red).
